title: International Shared Support
subtitle: Institutional backing, international participation, and shared continuity for Sutton SignWriting
summary: A support model for sustaining Sutton SignWriting as shared international language infrastructure rather than a single-maintainer project.
synopsis: Sutton SignWriting already supports international education, research, public language resources, and community use. This entry explains why support should combine an institutional home, international partner relationships, letters and statements of use, country-level coordination, and future grant, gift, donation, and collaboration pathways.

slides.title: Shared Language Infrastructure
slides.summary: Sutton SignWriting has international dependence, so its support model should be international too.
slides.speech: Sutton SignWriting is no longer only a personal project or a technical archive. It supports education, research, public language resources, and community use across countries. The support model should match that reality.
slides.notes: Open with infrastructure reality, not outreach rhetoric.

slides.title: Support Model
slides.summary: The model combines an institutional home, partner evidence, country coordination, and future funding pathways.
slides.speech: The strongest model combines an institutional home with international partner statements, country-level coordination, and future grants, gifts, donations, or collaboration pathways. No single partner has to carry the whole structure.
slides.notes: Make this feel practical and distributed.

slides.title: How Partners Help
slides.summary: Partners can strengthen the case through evidence, introductions, proposals, and local context.
slides.speech: International partners can help by documenting local use, writing letters of support, introducing appropriate institutional contacts, collaborating on proposals, and explaining what support would make the work more useful in their own setting.
